topic: A person finds out their article was published under someone else's name
raw_output:
[story 1]

The notification pinged on my phone, a stark intrusion into the quiet afternoon. My breath hitched as I clicked the link. The headline screamed my name, the accompanying text detailing the very piece of writing I had poured my soul into. My hands, already trembling slightly, began to shake as I scanned the byline. It wasn't mine. It was another person's name, a stranger’s name, attached to my words. A cold, sharp sensation shot down my spine, making the blood drain from my face. I reread the attribution, my eyes blurring slightly. Who? This wasn't just an oversight; this felt like a deliberate, calculated theft. I stood up abruptly, knocking my chair backward with a clatter that echoed in the empty room. My heart hammered against my ribs, a frantic drumbeat demanding an answer. I needed to check the original submission portal, needed to find out how this could have happened.

[story 2]

I stared at the screen, the pixels swimming before my eyes. The words I had painstakingly crafted, the unique insights I had wrestled from my own mind, were being presented as someone else's creation. A wave of nausea washed over me, making the room tilt precariously. I leaned closer to the monitor, my knuckles white where I gripped the edge of the desk. The implication hit me with the force of a physical blow: someone had taken my voice and used it for their own gain. A tight knot formed in my stomach, constricting my chest. I swallowed hard, the sound rasping in my throat. I started pacing the small space, my movements jerky and uncoordinated. Every shadow in the room seemed to shift, and I couldn't seem to focus on anything but the glaring discrepancy on the screen.

[story 3]

When the email arrived, I didn't move for a full minute. The air seemed to thicken, heavy and suffocating. I slowly opened the attachment, my gaze tracing the unfamiliar signature. It was a betrayal, raw and immediate. A sudden, intense heat flooded my cheeks, and I instinctively brought a hand up to touch my forehead, as if to ward off a fever. My jaw locked so tightly I could feel the strain in my molars. This wasn't just a mistake; this was a complete usurpation of my intellectual property. I stood rooted to the spot, listening to the distant sounds of the city, every nerve ending screaming with a need to confront whatever had just transpired. I took a deep, ragged breath, trying to steady the tremor that ran through my entire body.
